title: podman images <Repository> does not list images if present in locally
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

kind bug

podman images <Repository> when the image is a local image does not list the images in that repository. This is how docker works. I believe podman should have commandline compatibility with docker, if we want it to be a replacement for docker.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create any local image for example busybox

2. Run podman images <Local image repository> without giving the tag

**Describe the results you received:**
```
podman images localhost/busybox/base
unable to find 'localhost/busybox/base' in local storage: no such image
```
**Describe the results you expected:**
```
podman images localhost/busybox/base
REPOSITORY               TAG                 IMAGE ID            CREATED             SIZE
localhost/busybox/base   1.29.3              ade18c571e6f        4 days ago          2.65 MB
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```
Version:       1.0.0
Go Version:    go1.10.2
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
It is a Physical CentOS 7.5 machine with a custom built podman and updated shadow-utils. All the above commands ran as root. Did not try rootless, though I don't expect the behavior to be different.

comments.body: This is a bug ... it works with images tagged as latest ...

I actually now thing podman is behaving correcting, and certainly is behaving as docker does.  When the tag is omitted, the assumption is the tag is latest.  And therefore, localhost/busybox/base != localhost/busybox/base:1.29.3 and therefore should not be displayed.  @mtrmac do you agree with my assessment?
**docker-images**(1) explicitly documents that _repository_ without a _tag_ lists all images in the named repository.

… and this is what I see in my testing as well.

When naming a _single image_, the `:latest` tag tends to be assumed, but it’s up to every subcommand to define the syntax of semantics of its arguments, whether that is an image name, path to a local file, or a repository, or anything else. `docker images` just chose not treat its argument as a single image name.
@baude @mtrmac is this a bug or working as designed?
Well, **podman-images**(1) does not say, but if the intent is to be compatible with **docker-images**(1), it’s a bug.

Ok we should list all images then

```
docker pull fedora
docker pull fedora:26
 docker images fedora
REPOSITORY          TAG                 IMAGE ID            CREATED             SIZE
docker.io/fedora    latest              d7372e6c93c6        5 days ago          275 MB
docker.io/fedora    26                  f36d549d2474        5 days ago          232 MB
```

```
podman pull fedora
podman pull fedora:26
podman images fedora
REPOSITORY                 TAG      IMAGE ID       CREATED      SIZE
docker.io/library/fedora   latest   d7372e6c93c6   5 days ago   283 MB
```

Podman should have listed
docker.io/library/fedora   26    f36d549d2474   5 days ago   241 MB
